name: Jira Story Triage
description: Review a Jira backlog, sprint, or JQL-scoped set of work items for readiness to hand to engineering, and improve the weak ones. For every item that is not ready, the output explains why — which of the five-question bar's questions failed and the specific gap — not merely a tier label. On request, drafts the fix (acceptance criteria, a clearer outcome, a tighter scope) and writes it back to Jira via update-issue only after the user approves the exact drafted payload. Read-only until an approval; the write flow is opt-in and per-item.

deps._runtime_contract: Sibling skills are addressed BY NAME at runtime. The agent's harness resolves the name to whatever install location the user picked. `source` below is install guidance only — never a runtime path.

skills.name: jira
skills.purpose: All Jira reads: check, search (for the JQL scope), get-issue (image-content confirmation). Also the conditional update-issue call in the improve flow — invoked once per item only after the user approves the exact drafted payload. Hard dependency — there is no degraded path without it.

input.arguments: A JQL expression, a sprint/board scope, or a project key (defaults to open sprints). Optionally a repo URL or name for grounding.

artifacts: A reason-first Markdown readiness review: one row per item with an outcome (Ready for engineering / Gated / Not ready — needs shaping / Needs detail), complexity for Ready items, and a 'Why not ready' column naming the failed question(s) and specific gap. Followed by an opt-in improve flow that drafts fixes and writes them back via update-issue only after per-item approval.
